title: Neanotis prainiana
intro: Neanotis prainiana, also known as Indian snakeroot, is a flowering plant species in the family Rubiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine, exhibiting potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in treating various ailments like fever, respiratory issues, and skin conditions. Neanotis prainiana contains bioactive constituents including flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Indian medicine, Neanotis prainiana is commonly used to prepare herbal formulations like decoctions, infusions, and powdered extracts, which are often used to treat respiratory and fever-related conditions.
taxonomy: Neanotis prainiana belongs to the family Rubiaceae. This plant is a species of flowering plant in the genus Neanotis. Neanotis prainiana is native to the Himalayan region and parts of Asia. The plant is often used in traditional medicine due to its potential health benefits. Neanotis prainiana is also known for its unique flowers and small size.
